why: Selects mono-vs-stereo channel indexing (chan = channels>1 ? k%2 : 0) while decoding IMA ADPCM nibbles, so crafting a WAV/IMA-ADPCM file with fmt.nChannels=2 flips it into genuine stereo step-index interleaving.
Same mono/stereo channel-selection pattern for Microsoft ADPCM sample reconstruction, only reachable via a WAV file whose fmt chunk declares 2 channels for MS-ADPCM.

Gates whether a MARK chunk's pascal-string marker-name length fits the stack buffer directly from the file-supplied length byte, so an AIFF MARK entry with name length >= sizeof(scbuf)-1 flips it into the split-read overflow-guard path.
Checks whether the FLAC frame header's decoded channel count matches the stream's declared channel count, flippable by embedding a FLAC frame whose header channel field differs from STREAMINFO.
Validates the classic RIFF....WAVE magic tag right after the RIFF chunk, flippable by putting any 4-byte marker other than 'WAVE' after a RIFF header.
Detects an inconsistent AIFC COMM chunk where the compression tag is fl64/FL64 but sampleSize isn't 64, reachable by crafting an AIFC file with that exact tag/size mismatch.

Looks for the literal ASCII text 'channels_interleaved -s5 FALSE' in the NIST SPHERE text header to reject non-interleaved data, trivially flippable by writing that exact key/value string into the header.
Chunk-ID dispatch matching the CAF 'peak' chunk marker, only taken if the CAF file actually contains a peak-info sub-chunk, which then drives its own size-validation logic.
